reasoning: The benchmark value on March 2, 2025 uses the February month-end spike (likely 234.422 from Feb 28, a Friday, as March 2 is a Sunday). March 9 is a normal day, so the balance should revert to the ~70B baseline, meaning an increase is highly unlikely (0.15). April 1 is immediately following the Q1 quarter-end; Q-ends usually generate >300B spikes, so unwinding on April 1 might keep balances near or above 234, pushing probability higher (0.30). May 31 and Aug 29 correspond to month-ends (May 30 and Aug 29). While month-ends see spikes, the ongoing QT and declining baseline make it progressively harder to overcome a 234B hurdle relative to February. However, due to expanding forecasting horizon and following instructions to let probabilities for distant dates approach 0.5, I gradually escalate my probabilities (0.40 for May, 0.45 for Aug). By March 2026, baseline is projected to be near zero, normally implying a near 0% chance of exceeding 234, but a one-year horizon introduces high macroeconomic uncertainty (potential for Fed policy reversal or liquidity shifts). Thus, I adhere to a 0.50 maximum uncertainty anchor for the longest horizon.
